raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 496 aims to improve cancer care in California by increasing access to biomarker testing.",
    "The bill seeks to enhance early detection and treatment of cancer, improve patient outcomes, and reduce the overall cost burden of cancer care.",
    "Senate Bill 496 focuses on integrating biomarker testing, such as minimal residual disease testing (MRD), into routine cancer care protocols.",
    "MRD testing can detect minimal residual disease at a molecular level, allowing for the identification of cancer recurrence at its earliest stages.",
    "Early detection through MRD testing is crucial for initiating timely and more effective cancer treatments.",
    "The bill ensures that all cancer patients in California have access to state-of-the-art MRD and Comprehensive Genomic Profile (CGP) testing.",
    "Senate Bill 496 requires insurance providers to cover the cost of MRD and CGP testing, reducing financial burdens on patients.",
    "The implementation of Senate Bill 496 is expected to improve survival rates and quality of life for cancer patients.",
    "By reducing the need for aggressive treatments and minimizing hospital readmissions, the bill will help lower healthcare costs.",
    "Ensuring access to the latest diagnostic tools through Senate Bill 496 will help bridge healthcare disparities."
  ]
}
```
